summary: Phase 3 frontend testing of DXF/CAD Viewer and RAR Archive Viewer. All pages load correctly. Core functionality works but DXF SVG geometry is invisible due to black stroke on dark canvas.

ui_bugs.issue: DXF library (dxf v5.3.1) generates SVG with stroke='rgb(0,0,0)' (black), but canvas background is bg-slate-950 (near-black). Result: all CAD geometry is invisible — black lines on black background. SVG IS rendered in DOM (confirmed) but visually blank.

issues: SVG strokes from dxf library default to black (rgb(0,0,0)). Dark canvas (slate-950) makes lines invisible. Fix: either (a) change canvas background to white/light, or (b) after calling helper.toSVG(), replace all stroke='rgb(0,0,0)' and stroke='#000000' with white (#ffffff or stroke='currentColor' with text-white on canvas).

action_items: CadViewer.tsx: Fix SVG visibility on dark canvas. The dxf library generates black strokes (stroke='rgb(0,0,0)'). Fix options: (1) Change canvas bg from bg-slate-950 to bg-white and remove dark theme, OR (2) post-process svgStr to replace black stroke/fill with white — e.g., svgStr.replace(/stroke="rgb\(0,\s*0,\s*0\)"/g, 'stroke="#00ff88"').replace(/stroke="#000000"/g, 'stroke="#00ff88"'). A teal/green color on dark bg would match the CAD aesthetic.
critical_code_review_comments: CadViewer.tsx line 109: bg-slate-950 (dark canvas) + SVG black strokes from dxf library = invisible geometry. The dark CAD aesthetic is intentional but requires post-processing SVG stroke colors to be light/visible.

ArchiveViewer.tsx: Correctly handles both ZIP and RAR via extension detection. WASM loaded successfully from /unrar.wasm. With minimal 8-byte RAR header, shows 'Arşiv boş görünüyor' (empty archive) - correct behavior.
viewer-registry.ts: DXF and RAR correctly registered. DXF uses 'cad' engine, RAR uses 'archive' engine (shared with ZIP via extension detection in ArchiveViewer).

success_rate.frontend: 87% (7/8 features working: pages load, file upload works, RAR WASM loads, ZIP still works, hub shows DXF+RAR, size limit guard works. 1 issue: DXF geometry invisible on dark canvas)

seed_data_creation: Created /tmp/test.dxf (minimal DXF R12 with LINE and CIRCLE), /tmp/test.zip (with 2 text files). RAR creation tools not available - tested with 8-byte RAR5 magic header via JS injection.
context_for_next_testing_agent: DXF page: /en/view/dxf — uploads work, SVG renders in DOM but invisible (black on black). Fix the stroke color post-processing in CadViewer.tsx then verify by uploading /tmp/test.dxf and checking the dark canvas shows visible geometry. RAR page: /en/view/rar — WASM loads OK, tested with stub RAR showing 'empty archive'. ZIP viewer: /en/view/zip — fully working. Viewer hub: /en/view — both DXF and RAR visible in category grid.

DXF_invisible_geometry.issue: DXF file uploads successfully, SVG is generated and injected into DOM, but geometry is invisible
DXF_invisible_geometry.root_cause: The 'dxf' npm package's Helper.toSVG() generates SVG with stroke='rgb(0,0,0)' for default layer 0 (black color index). CadViewer uses bg-slate-950 dark canvas. Black strokes on near-black background = zero contrast.
DXF_invisible_geometry.fix: In CadViewer.tsx, after `const svgStr: string = helper.toSVG()`, add post-processing: `const visibleSvg = svgStr.replace(/stroke="rgb\(0,\s*0,\s*0\)"/g, 'stroke="#4ade80"').replace(/stroke="#000000"/g, 'stroke="#4ade80"')` then `setSvg(visibleSvg)`. Alternatively change canvas bg to white.
DXF_invisible_geometry.reproduction: Upload /tmp/test.dxf to /en/view/dxf — dark canvas shows completely black, no visible lines or circles despite SVG being injected with LINE/CIRCLE paths
